session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 9372 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute it with the specified arguments.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Classify PR review feedback by filling in semantic fields from mechanical CLI output. 16→ 17→## Steps 18→ 19→1. **Run mechanical classification:** 20→ 21→ ```bash 22→ erk exec classify-pr-feedback [--pr <number>] [--include-resolved] 23→ ``` 24→ 25→ Pass `--pr <number>` if specified in `$ARGUMENTS`. Pass `--include-resolved` if specified in `$ARGUMENTS`. 26→ 27→ This returns JSON with mechanically pre-classified items: 28→ - **review_submissions**: PR-level reviews (classification: "actionable" | "informational" | "needs_llm") 29→ - **review_threads**: Inline review threads (pre_existing_candidate flag set for bot + restructured) 30→ - **discussion_comments**: Discussion comments (classification: "informational" | "needs_llm")
... [169 lines omitted] ...
104→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 106→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 144→ "error": null 159→## Error Case 173→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 191→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill by running the mechanical classification step and then performing the semantic analysis.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run mechanical PR feedback classification for PR 9372

{"author":{"login":"github-actions"},"authorAssociation":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"<!-- tripwires-review -->

## ✅ Tripwires Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-21 09:26:59 PT

Found 0 violations across 0 files. No tripwire violations detected in this systematic terminology rename.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Tripwires Checked

This PR was reviewed against all category tripwires:
- ✅ Universal tripwires (architecture, gateways, subprocess)
- ✅ Architecture tripwires (200 rules)
- ✅ CLI tripwires (114 rules)
- ✅ Planning tripwires (128 rules)
- ✅ Testing tripwires (69 rules)
- ✅ TUI tripwires (63 rules)

### Review Scope

Changed files reviewed:
- Documentation files (learned docs): 9 files
- Python implementation files: 16 files
- Test files: 8 files

### Violations Summary

No violations found.

This PR performs systematic terminology renaming from "plan" to "pr" in:
- Function names (`_has_plan_title_prefix` → `_has_pr_title_prefix`)
- Help text and documentation
- Internal comments
- Variable names

All changes are mechanical renames with no new business logic, architectural changes, or tripwire-triggering patterns.

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-21 09:26:59 PT**: No tripwire violations detected in systematic terminology rename from "plan" to "pr"

{"author":{"login":"github-actions"},"authorAssociation":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"<!-- audit-pr-docs -->

## ❌ Audit PR Docs

**Last updated:** 2026-03-21 09:29:40 PT

Found 3 violations across 2 files. Inline comments posted for each.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ Verbatim code blocks — None found (template blocks in remote-workflow-template.md are TEMPLATE classification, not VERBATIM)
❌ Inaccurate claims — Found in docs/learned/planning/metadata-update-patterns.md and docs/learned/erk/remote-workflow-template.md
✅ Duplicative sections — None found
✅ Drift risk (symbol renames) — PR correctly updates all symbol references

### Violations Summary
- `docs/learned/planning/metadata-update-patterns.md:43`: Set-based field validation claim does not match implementation (no such validation exists); also references `resolve_plan_id_for_branch()` (should be `resolve_pr_number_for_branch()`)
- `docs/learned/planning/metadata-update-patterns.md:9`: Tripwire says "silent skip with warning" but function silently returns with no warning; also `write_dispatch_metadata()` described as calling `get_plan()`/`PlanNotFound` (actually `get_managed_pr()`/`PrNotFound`)
- `docs/learned/erk/remote-workflow-template.md:81`: Pre-existing: line 86 claims "Extracts plan number from `P{number}-*` branch names" — legacy format, current code uses `plnd/` prefix

### Files Reviewed
| File | Verdict | Duplicative % | High Value % | Issues |
|------|---------|---------------|-------------|--------|
| `docs/learned/architecture/unified-dispatch-pattern.md` | KEEP | 5% | 85% | 0 (clean rename) |
| `docs/learned/cli/workflow-commands.md` | KEEP | 5% | 80% | 0 (clean rename) |
| `docs/learned/erk/remote-workflow-template.md` | SIMPLIFY | 15% | 60% | 1 inaccurate (pre-existing, line 86) |
| `docs/learned/planning/index.md` | KEEP | 0% | 100% | 0 (auto-generated) |
| `docs/learned/planning/metadata-update-patterns.md` | SIMPLIFY | 10% | 60% | 2 inaccurate |
| `docs/learned/planning/one-shot-workflow.md` | KEEP | 5% | 85% | 0 (clean rename) |
| `docs/learned/planning/tripwires.md` | KEEP | 0% | 100% | 0 (auto-generated) |

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-21 09:29:40 PT**: Audited 7 docs. Found 3 inaccurate claims: metadata-update-patterns.md has stale implementation description (non-existent set validation, wrong function names); remote-workflow-template.md has pre-existing stale branch pattern claim (P{number}-* vs plnd/). Renames in other 5 docs are clean.
